name: UPAY
business: UPAY provides cloud-based loan management software (ACPAS), anti-money laundering compliance software (AML GO), and an online hunting marketplace with installment financing (HUNTPAL) primarily to customers in South Africa and the United States.

exposures.sentence: We provide a cloud-based loan origination software system that is compliant with all applicable legislation and enables our customers to grant loans, sell products, pay bills or pay monthly subscriptions on terms, all within our software system.
HUNTPAL LLC, headquartered in the United States, operates an online Hunt Now Pay Later marketplace that connects licensed hunting outfitters with US hunters.
Acting as an appointed agent for a licensed insurer, we embed credit protection and life-insurance offerings directly into ACPAS.

catalysts.event: Launch of AML compliance and related services in the United States
catalysts.sentence: The Company is currently evaluating potential expansion opportunities into the United States market and anticipates launching certain AML and compliance-related services in the United States within the next six months, although no assurances can be given regarding the timing or success of such expansion efforts.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. check-cashing and payday-loan services industry revenue of $21.4 billion in 2023
tamClaims.sentence: According to IBISWorld, the U.S. check-cashing and payday-loan services industry generated approximately $21.4 billion in revenue during 2023, reflecting continued consumer demand for short-term and alternative credit products and related financial services.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. outdoor recreation economy output of $1.1 trillion
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Outdoor Industry Association and the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis Outdoor Recreation Satellite Account, the U.S. outdoor recreation economy generated approximately $1.1 trillion in economic output in recent years, while hunting-related activities contributed tens of billions of dollars annually to the U.S. economy through equipment sales, travel expenditures, outfitter services, and tourism-related spending.

tamClaims.claim: Total value of new credit granted in South Africa in Q4 2024 was approximately R158.70 billion
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Credit Regulator's Consumer Credit Market Report for Q4 2024, the total value of new credit granted in South Africa increased to approximately R158.70 billion for the quarter ended December 2024.
